compiled_state.title: As a hook developer, I can source a shared ERR handler that writes enriched JSONL to a canonical log path
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Creates `hooks/lib/hook-error-handler.sh` with `_dso_hook_err_handler()` — a shared bash function that any fail-open hook can register as its ERR trap. Establishes the canonical log path `~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl`. Updates the sole reader (`session-safety-check.sh`) to read from the new path while also reading the legacy path during the migration window. Updates all test fixtures to use the new path.

**Why**: The existing ~24 copy-pasted inline logging traps are the divergence point: every trap reinvents the same logic with only the hook name changed. A shared handler eliminates this divergence and creates the foundation that Stories S2–S4 build on.

**Scope**:
- IN: `hooks/lib/hook-error-handler.sh` with `_dso_hook_err_handler()`; handler disables ERR trap inside body, uses only builtins, caches plugin version on first source, includes log size guard, falls back gracefully for git/path failures; new log path `~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl`; `session-safety-check.sh` updated to read both new path and legacy path during migration window; `tests/hooks/test-session-safety.sh` and `tests/hooks/test-session-misc-no-jq.sh` updated to use new log path; new test verifying ERR trigger produces correct JSONL structure with all enriched fields
- OUT: Migrating any existing hooks to use the handler (Stories S2–S4); creating the `~/.claude/logs/` directory on user machines (created lazily on first write)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `hooks/lib/hook-error-handler.sh` exists with `_dso_hook_err_handler()` that can be sourced from any bash hook and registers itself as an ERR trap
  ← Satisfies: "A shared _dso_hook_err_handler function exists in hooks/lib/hook-error-handler.sh that all fail-open hooks use"
- When this story is complete, triggering an ERR in a bash context that has registered the shared handler writes a JSONL entry to `~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl` containing all enriched fields: ts, hook, line, repo_root, plugin_version, bash_version, os
  ← Satisfies: "Error logs written to ~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl with enriched fields: timestamp, hook name, line number, repo root, plugin version, bash version, OS"
- When this story is complete, `session-safety-check.sh` reads from both `~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl` (new path) and `~/.claude/hook-error-log.jsonl` (legacy path), merging results so no errors are lost during the migration window before S2–S4 complete
  ← Satisfies: "All existing tests pass"
- When this story is complete, `tests/hooks/test-session-safety.sh` and `tests/hooks/test-session-misc-no-jq.sh` both use the new log path `~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl` in their fixtures
  ← Satisfies: "All existing tests pass"
- When this story is complete, at least one test confirms that triggering an ERR in a function registered with the handler produces a JSONL entry with all 7 enriched fields in the correct format
  ← Satisfies: "At least one new test verifies ERR trigger produces correctly-structured JSONL entry with all enriched fields"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] ERR trap inheritance requires `set -E` (errtrace) to be active in the sourcing script; without it, traps registered at the top level do not fire inside functions. Handler contract should require `set -E` in sourcing scripts and optionally include a self-check that warns if errtrace is off.
- [Reliability] The `~/.claude/logs/` directory may not exist on user machines — the handler must create it lazily (e.g., `mkdir -p`) before first write, using `|| true` to preserve fail-open
- [Testing] New log path `~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl` requires the test harness to use an isolated `TEST_HOME` directory — verify test fixtures use `$TEST_HOME` not literal `$HOME`

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

STATUS:complete

Implementation plan for cf83-514c complete.

Tasks:
- T1: 6235-23e6 — Write RED test for hook-error-handler.sh (testing_mode: RED)
- T2: 4fd9-7d9c — Implement hooks/lib/hook-error-handler.sh (testing_mode: RED)
- T3: 4444-6bb6 — Write UPDATE test for session-safety-check.sh dual-read (testing_mode: UPDATE)
- T4: d6db-deeb — Migrate session-safety-check.sh to shared handler + dual-read (testing_mode: UPDATE)

Dependency chain: T1 → T2 → T3 → T4 (linear)
